author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. vii. 7, ‘Ask, and it shall be given you;’ etc. An exhortation to alms-deeds.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. vii. 7, ‘Ask, and it shall be given you;’ etc. An exhortation to alms-deeds.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the lesson of the Holy Gospel the Lord hath exhorted us to prayer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Ask,” saith He, “and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For every one that asketh receiveth, and he that seeketh findeth, and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or what man is there of you, whom if his son ask bread, will he give him a stone?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or if he ask a fish, will he give him a serpent?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or if he ask an egg, will he offer him a scorpion?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If ye then,” saith He, “though ye be evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father which is in heaven give good things to them that ask Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Though ye be evil,” He saith, “ye know how to give good gifts unto your children.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A marvellous thing, Brethren! we are evil: yet have we a good Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is more evident?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard our proper name: “Though ye be evil, ye know how to give good gifts unto your children.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And now see what kind of Father He showeth them, whom he called evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “How much more shall your Father?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Father of whom? undoubtedly of the evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what kind of Father?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “None is good but God only.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this cause have we who are evil a good Father, that we may not always continue evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No evil man can make another man good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If no evil man can make another good, how can an evil man make himself good?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He only can make of an evil man a good man, who is good eternally.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Heal me, and I shall be healed; save me, and I shall be saved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then do those vain ones say to me in words vain as themselves, “Thou canst save thyself if thou wilt”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Heal me, O Lord, and I shall be healed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We were created good by The Good; for “God made man upright,” but by our own free will, we became evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We had power from being good to become evil, and we shall have power from being evil to become good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But it is He who is ever Good, who maketh the good out of the evil; for man by his own will had no power to heal himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost not look out for a physician to wound thyself; but when thou hast wounded thyself, thou lookest out for one to cure thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Good things then after the time present, temporal good things, such as are concerned with the body and flesh, we do know how to give to our children, even though we are evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For even these are good things, who would doubt it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A fish, an egg, bread, fruit, wheat, the light we see, the air we breathe, all these are good; the very riches by which men are lifted up, and which make them loth to acknowledge other men to be their equals; by which, I say, men are lifted up rather in love of their dazzling clothing, than with any thought of their common nature, even these riches, I repeat, are good; but all these goods which I have now mentioned may be possessed by good and bad alike; and though they be good themselves, yet cannot they make their owners good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A good then there is which maketh good, and a good there is whereby thou mayest do good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Good which maketh good is God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For none can make man good, save He who is Good eternally.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore that thou mayest be good, call upon God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But there is another good whereby thou mayest do good, and that is, whatever thou mayest possess.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is gold, there is silver; they are good, not such as can make thee good, but whereby thou mayest do good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast gold and silver, and thou desirest more gold and silver.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou both hast, and desirest to have; thou art at once full, and thirsty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is a disease, not opulence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When men are in the dropsy, they are full of water, and yet are always thirsty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are full of water, and yet they thirst for water.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then canst thou take pleasure in opulence, who hast thereby this dropsical desire?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Gold then thou hast, it is good; yet thou hast not whereby thou canst be made good, but whereby thou canst do good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou ask, What good can I do with gold?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hast thou not heard in the Psalm, “He hath dispersed abroad, he hath given to the poor, his righteousness remaineth for ever.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is good, this is the good whereby thou art made good; righteousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou have the good whereby thou art made good, do good with that good which cannot make thee good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast money, deal it out freely.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By dealing it out freely, thou increasest righteousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For he hath dispersed abroad, hath distributed, hath given to the poor; his righteousness remaineth for ever.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See what is diminished and what increased.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy money is diminished, thy righteousness increased.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That is diminished which thou must soon have lost, that diminished which thou must soon have left behind thee; that increased which thou shalt possess for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is then a secret of gainful dealing I am giving; learn so to trade.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou dost commend the merchant who selleth lead and getteth gold, and wilt thou not commend the merchant, who layeth out money, and getteth righteousness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou wilt say, I do not lay out my money, because I have not righteousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him who has righteousness lay his money out; I have not righteousness, so at least let me have my money.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou not then wish to lay out thy money, because thou hast not righteousness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, lay it out then rather that thou mayest have righteousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For from whence shalt thou have righteousness but from God, the Fountain of righteousness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, if thou wilt have righteousness, be God’s beggar, who just now out of the Gospel urged thee to ask, and seek, and knock.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He knew His beggar, and lo the Householder, the mighty rich One, rich, to wit, in riches spiritual and eternal, exhorteth thee and saith, “Ask, seek, knock; he that asketh receiveth, he that seeketh findeth, to him that knocketh it shall be opened.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He exhorteth thee to ask, and will he refuse thee what thou askest?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider a similitude or comparison drawn from a contrary case (as of that unjust judge), which is an encouragement to us to prayer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “There was,” saith the Lord, “in a city a certain judge, which feared not God, neither regarded man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A certain widow importuned him daily, and said, “Avenge me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He would not for a long time; but she ceased not to petition, and he did through her importunity what he would not of his own good will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thus by a contrary case hath He recommended us to pray.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, He saith, “A certain man to whom some guest had come, went to his friend, and began to knock and say, A guest is come to me, lend me three loaves.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He answered, “I am already in bed, and my servants with me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The other does not leave off, but stands and presses his case, and knocks and begs as one friend of another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what saith He?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I say unto you that he riseth, and not because of his friendship,” but “because of the other’s importunity he giveth him as many as he wanted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not because of his friendship,” though he is his friend, but “because of his importunity.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the meaning of “because of his importunity?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because he did not leave off knocking; because even when his request was refused, he did not turn away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who was not willing to give, gave what was asked, because the other fainted not in asking.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How much more then shall that Good One give who exhorteth us to ask, who is displeased if we ask not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when at times He giveth somewhat slowly, it is that He is showing us the value of His good things; not that He refuses them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Things which have been long desired, are obtained with the greater pleasure, whereas those which are given quickly, are held cheap.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ask then, seek, be instant.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By the very asking and seeking thou dost grow so as to contain the more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God is keeping in reserve for thee, what it is not His will to give thee quickly, that thou mayest learn for great things to long with great desire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore “ought we always to pray, and not to faint.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then God hath made us His beggars by admonishing, and exhorting, and commanding us to ask, and seek, and knock, let us for our part pay regard to those who ask from us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We ask, and from whom do we ask?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who are we that ask?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What do we ask?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From whom, or who are we, or what is it that we ask?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We ask of the Good God; and we that ask are evil men; but we ask for righteousness, whereby we may be good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We ask then for that which we may have for ever, wherewith when we shall be filled, we shall want no more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in order that we may be filled, let us hunger and thirst; hungering and thirsting, let us ask, and seek, and knock.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For blessed are they who hunger and thirst after righteousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore are they blessed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They do hunger and thirst, and are they blessed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is want ever a blessing?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are not blessed in that they hunger and thirst, but in that they will be filled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There will there be blessedness, in the fulness, not in the hunger.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But hunger must go before the fulness, that no loathing attach to the bread.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have said then, from whom it is that we ask, and who we are that ask, and what we ask.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But we also are asked ourselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we are God’s mendicants; that He may acknowledge His mendicants, let us on our part acknowledge ours.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But let us think in this case again, when anything is asked of us, who they are that ask, from whom they ask, and what they ask?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who then are they that ask?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From whom do they ask?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who are they that ask?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From mortals.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who are they that ask?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Frail beings.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From frail beings.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who are they that ask?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And from whom?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From wretches.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Excepting in the matter of wealth, they that ask are as they of whom they ask.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With what face canst thou ask before thy lord, who dost not acknowledge thine own equal?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I am not,” he will say, “as he is,” far be it from me to be such as he.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is thus that one clad in silk, and puffed up with pride, speaks of one who is wrapped in rags.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But I ask you when you both are stripped.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask you not as you are now when clothed, but as you were when you were first born.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Both were naked, both weak, beginning a life of misery, and therefore beginning it with cries.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See then, recall, O rich man, to mind thy first beginnings; see whether thou broughtest anything into the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now thou hast come indeed, and hast found so great abundance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But tell me, I pray thee, what didst thou bring hither?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell me, or if thou art ashamed to say, hear the Apostle.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “We brought nothing into this world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He saith, “We brought nothing into this world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But perhaps because thou broughtest in nothing, but yet hast found much here, thou wilt take away something hence?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This too, peradventure through love of riches, thou art afraid to confess.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear this also, and let the Apostle who will not flatter, tell thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “We brought nothing into this world,” to wit when we were born; “neither can we carry anything out,” to wit when we shall depart out of the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou broughtest in nothing, and thou shalt carry nothing away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then dost thou puff up thyself against the poor man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When infants first are born, let only the parents, servants, dependants, and the crowds of obsequious attendants, get out of the way; and then let the wealthy children with their cries be recognised.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the rich woman and the poor give birth together; let them take no notice of their children, let them go away for a little while; then let them return, and recognise them if they can.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See then, O rich man, “thou broughtest nothing into this world; neither canst thou carry anything out.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What I have said of them at their birth, I may say of them in death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If it be not so, when by any chance old sepulchres are broken up, let the bones of the rich be recognised if they can.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, thou rich man, give ear to the Apostle, “We brought nothing into this world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Acknowledge it, true it is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Neither can we carry anything out.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Acknowledge it, this is true also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What follows then?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Having food and covering, let us be therewith content; for they who wish to be rich fall into temptation, and many and hurtful lusts, which drown men in destruction and perdition.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For avarice is the root of all evil, which some following after, have erred from the faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now consider what they have abandoned.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Grieved thou art that they have abandoned this, but see now in what they have entangled themselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear; “They have erred from the faith, and entangled themselves in many sorrows.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “They who wish to be rich.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is one thing to be rich, another to wish to become rich.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is rich, who is born of rich parents, and he is rich not because he wished it, but because many left him their inheritances.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: His wealth I see, I make no question as to the pleasure he takes in it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this Scripture it is covetousness that is condemned, not gold, or silver, or riches, but covetousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they who do not wish to become rich, or do not care about it, who do not burn with covetous desires, nor are inflamed by the fires of avarice, but who yet are rich, let them hear the Apostle (it has been read to-day), “Charge them that are rich in this world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Charge them what?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Charge them before all things, not to be proud in their conceits, for there is nothing which riches do so much generate as pride.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Each several fruit, each several grain of corn, each several tree, has its peculiar worm, and the worm of the apple is of one kind, and of the pear another, and of the bean another, and of the wheat another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The worm of riches is pride.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Charge therefore the rich of this world that they be not proud in their conceits.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath shut out the abuse, let him teach now the proper use.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That they be not proud in their conceits.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whence cometh the defence against pride?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From that which follows: “Nor trust in the uncertainty of riches.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who trust not in the uncertainty of riches, are not proud in their conceits.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If they be not proud in their conceits, let them fear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If they fear, they are not proud in their conceits.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How many are they who were rich yesterday, and are poor to-day?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How many go to sleep rich, and through robbers coming and taking all away, wake up poor?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore “charge them not to trust in the uncertainty of riches, but in the Living God, who giveth us richly all things to enjoy,” things temporal, and things eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But things eternal more for enjoyment, the things temporal for use.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Things temporal as for travellers, things eternal as for inhabitants.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Things temporal, whereby we may do good; things eternal, whereby we may be made good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore let the rich do this, “Let them not be proud in their conceits, nor trust in the uncertainty of riches, but in the Living God, who giveth us all things richly to enjoy.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them do this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what can they do with what they have?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let them be rich in good works, let them easily distribute.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they have wherewithal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then do they not do it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Poverty is a hard estate.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they may give easily, for they have the means.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let them communicate,” that is, let them acknowledge their fellow-mortals as their equals.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let them communicate, let them lay up for themselves a good foundation against the time to come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, saith he, when I say, “Let them distribute easily, let them communicate,” I have no wish to spoil, or strip them, or leave them empty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is a painful lesson I teach; I show them a place to put their goods, “let them lay up in store for themselves.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I have no wish that they should remain in poverty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let them lay up for themselves in store.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do not bid them lose their goods, but I show them whither to remove them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let them lay up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may hold on the true life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The present then is a false life; let them lay hold on the true life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For it is vanity of vanities, and all is vanity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What so great abundance hath man in all his labour, wherewith he laboureth under the sun?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore the true life must be laid hold upon, our riches must be removed to the place of the true life, that we may find there what we give here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He maketh this exchange of our goods who also changeth ourselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give then, my brethren, to the poor, “Having food and covering, let us be therewith content.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The rich man has nothing from his riches, but what the poor man begs of him, food and covering.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What more hast thou from all that thou possessest?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast got food and necessary covering.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Necessary I say, not useless, not superfluous.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What more dost thou get from thy riches?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Assuredly all thou hast more will be superfluous.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let thy superfluities then be the poor man’s necessaries.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou wilt say, I get costly banquets, I feed on costly meats.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the poor man, what does he feed on?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On cheap food; the poor man feeds on cheap, and I, says he, on costly meats.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well, I ask you, when you both are filled, the costly enters into thee, but when it is once entered, what does it become?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we had but looking-glasses within us, should we not be put to shame for all the costly meat whereby thou hast been filled?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The poor man hungers, and so does the rich; the poor man seeks to be filled, so does the rich.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The poor man is filled with inexpensive, the rich with costly meats.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Both are filled alike, the object whither both wish to attain is one and the same, only the one reaches it by a short, the other by a circuitous way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou wilt say, I relish better my costly food.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: True, and it is hard for thee to be satisfied, dainty as thou art.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou knowest not the relish of that which hunger seasons.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not that I have said this to force the rich to feed on the meat and drink of the poor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the rich use what their infirmity has accustomed them to; but let them be sorry, that they are not able to do otherwise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it would be better for them if they could.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then the poor man be not puffed up for his poverty, why shouldest thou for thine infirmity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Use then choice, and costly meats, because thou art so accustomed, because thou canst not do otherwise, because if thou dost change thy custom, thou art made ill.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I grant thee this, make use of superfluities, but give to the poor necessaries; make use of costly meats, but give to the poor inexpensive food.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is looking to receive from thee, and thou art looking to receive from God; he is looking to the hand which was made as he was, and thou art looking to the hand that made thee, and made not thee only, but the poor man with thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He set you both one and the same journey, this present life: you have found yourselves companions in it, you are walking one way: he is carrying nothing, thou art loaded excessively: he is carrying nothing with him, thou art carrying with thee more than thou dost need.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art loaded: give him of that thou hast; so shalt thou at once feed him, and lessen thine own burden.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give then to the poor; I beg, I advise, I charge, I command you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give to the poor whatever ye will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I will not conceal from you, Beloved, why it is that I have deemed it necessary to deliver this discourse to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As I am going to and from the Church, the poor importune me, and beg me to speak to you, that they may receive something of you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They have urged me to speak to you; and when they see that they receive nothing from you, they suppose that all my labour among you is in vain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Something also they expect from me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I give them all I can; but have I the means sufficient to supply all their necessities?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Forasmuch then as I have not means sufficient to supply all their necessity, I am at least their ambassador to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You have heard and applauded; God be thanked.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You have received the seed, you have returned an answer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But these your commendations weigh me down rather, and expose me to danger.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I bear them, and tremble whilst I bear them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless, my brethren, these your commendations are but the tree’s leaves; it is the fruit I am in quest of.
